babyDevelopment: The baby is becoming more sensitive to light and sound. The head is growing larger to accommodate the rapidly developing brain. Muscles and lungs are continuing to mature. The baby might start positioning itself head-down.
bodyChanges: You may feel more uncomfortable as the uterus pushes into your ribs. Constipation and gas might be more frequent. Your breasts may start leaking colostrum (the first milk), which is normal preparation for nursing.
tips: Incorporate more calcium for baby's bone development
Keep up with your kick counts

do: Drink at least 2.5L of water daily
Use absorbent pads in your bra if leaking colostrum
Focus on deep breathing during activities
dont: Lift heavy objects that put strain on your back

travelAdvisory.note: Short trips are safer; carry medical records.

milestoneDescription: Baby's brain development causes the head to grow significantly this week.
